title: Bitter Polish Peppadew Peppers Bake
description: A unique Polish-inspired dish featuring sweet Peppadew peppers in a salt-baked crust. Perfect for adventurous eaters on a Whole30 diet.
instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and set aside.
In a medium bowl, whisk together melted coconut oil, minced garlic, chopped parsley, chopped dill, salt, and black pepper.
Add the Peppadew peppers to the bowl and toss to coat with the garlic mixture.
In a separate bowl, whisk together apple cider vinegar, honey, and melted coconut oil.
Brush the peppadew peppers with the vinegar mixture, leaving some of the garlic mixture exposed.
Dip the edges of the almond flour in the eggs and coat the Peppadew peppers evenly with the almond flour mixture.
Place the Peppadew peppers on the prepared baking sheet, leaving some space between each pepper.
Bake for 20 minutes or until the crust is golden brown and crispy.
Remove the peppers from the oven and let them cool slightly before serving.
Garnish with chopped parsley or dill, if desired.
